summary: Patrick McKenzie shares his mid-October sales statistics for Bingo Card Creator, his micro-ISV product. He reports 13 units sold with one return, generating $311.59 in net sales against $105.02 in expenses for a net profit of $206.57. He notes business is stronger than the previous month and sets a target of 25-30 copies and $500 profit for the full month. A planned Halloween promotion has been delayed due to his day job commitments.
key_points: Mid-month net profit of $206.57 puts him on track to exceed previous months' performance
Expenses remain low at ~$105/month split between hosting, e-commerce platform, and AdWords
The tension between day job and side business is limiting his ability to execute marketing promotions
He is tracking toward 25-30 unit sales for the month, showing steady growth in the micro-ISV
money_quotes: Business this month is going substantially stronger than last month.
The Halloween promotion I was hoping to do has been mostly eclipsed by my work at work, but we'll see if I can't get something figured out this weekend.
key_insight: Even with minimal marketing effort and day-job constraints, a well-positioned micro-ISV can generate consistent and growing monthly profit from a simple software product.
